name: Persist repositories with transactional scope joins
outcome: Auth-code and token repositories persist the row AND its scope links in one transaction, and load scopes back on read, fixing the dropped-scopes bug.
scope: auth_code_repository and token_repository on Drizzle: issue/persist/getByIdentifier/getByRefreshToken/issueRefreshToken/revoke, wiring oauthAuthCodeScopes and oauthTokenScopes.
acceptance_criteria: AuthCodeRepository.persist writes the auth code and its scope rows to oauthAuthCodeScopes in a single transaction
TokenRepository.persist writes the token and its scope rows to oauthTokenScopes in a single transaction; getByRefreshToken/findById load scopes
issueRefreshToken and revoke preserve current behavior (rotate refresh token; revoke via expiry/flag)
steps: src/app/oauth/repositories/auth_code_repository.ts — persist: db.transaction inserting the auth code row then its oauthAuthCodeScopes links; getByIdentifier includes client and scopes; keep isRevoked/revoke
src/app/oauth/repositories/token_repository.ts — persist: db.transaction inserting the token row then its oauthTokenScopes links; issueToken/issueRefreshToken preserve intervals and rotation
src/app/oauth/repositories/token_repository.ts — getByRefreshToken/findById load client, user, and scopes; revoke updates the row
tests — assert persist writes scope join rows and getByIdentifier/getByRefreshToken return those scopes; assert a token persisted with two scopes reads back both
notes: This is the core of the scope-persistence fix (ADR-0001). getByIdentifier previously omitted scopes entirely; loading them is required for the auth-code->token exchange and refresh narrowing to carry real scopes. finalize is NOT changed.
